feed.title: 4chan
feed.subtitle: Newest threads from selected worksafe 4chan boards (news, g, o, tv, v, mu, vip) via the read-only JSON API, plus the official 4chan blog. Board posts are user-generated and unmoderated; each title is prefixed with its board.

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: Physical games are bad for the environment
entry.content: Physical games are bad for the environment

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: >Altogether, more than 2,000,000 people were killed in Belarus during the three …
entry.content: >Altogether, more than 2,000,000 people were killed in Belarus during the three years of Nazi occupation, around 25% of the region's population,[1][2] or even as high as 3,000,000 killed or 30% of the population,[3][4] with an estimation that 1 in 3 Belarusians died during WW2.[5] In total, on the territory of modern Belarus, more than 9,200 villages and settlements, and 682,000 buildings were destroyed and burned, with some settlements burned several times.[6] By the end of the war, Belarus had

entry.title: /g/ Technology: They replace the button you want with the microphone button
entry.content: They replace the button you'd normally click on with this. They put it wherever you'd usually click on. Whatever button (submit/search/send etc) you were meaning to click has been replaced with this one. They replace the button you wanted to click on with the microphone button. The microphone button is now in the spot where the button you had expected was before.

entry.title: /int/ International: I want to look Mexican
entry.content: How do I look and act Mexican? I obviously need to become more extroverted and learn Spanish but honestly I'm too pale to look Mexican and I don't take well. entry.title: /int/ International: Something i've learned from Anglo-Saxon tourists is they think the whole world s…
entry.content: Something i've learned from Anglo-Saxon tourists is they think the whole world speak english or should speak english. They can't comprehend people not speaking it. They frown and leave once they see you don't speak it, like they're genuinely upset from it. So now i always pretend not to speak english so i don't have to interact with them in town, and it's funny to see them mad. I want every angloid to have a shit time here. Get the fuck out of Nice you pale ass fat fucking poof.

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: This game is fun as fuck what the fuck???
entry.content: Why do people hate Dirge of Cerberus like crazy? The gameplay is alright, the story is fine, it's not any more whacky or nonsensical or tranime than actual FF 7. So why is it so hated? My only complain is that it's like 8 hours long. It would have been perfect if it was 4 or 6. 8 is too much.

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: /tv/ zeitgeist
entry.content: Post any 4 sequential images from your phone's /tv/ image bank within the last 30 days. This is for a research project to understand better shitposting dynamics. Go. It will be fun.

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: 3x3 - 10x10 Thread (validate my taste ... plz)
entry.content: Another one of these, but talk about the games you love or perhaps hate. You can even make a 3x3 for games you dislike instead this time around. I've been playing the Xenia version of WET lately and it emulates at a solid 80-90 FPS, it's a fuckin' dream. Been also grinding through the other GTAs, finished 1 and 2, finished 3, now I'm starting Vice City, with San Andreas soon to be next. 2 is the best IMO, but 3 has it's charm and moments. Claude Speed FTW. https://topsters.org/

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: I literally do not care about permanent ownership of games. I think of them like…
entry.content: I literally do not care about permanent ownership of games. I think of them like food or a vacation experience, I consume them then move on with my life, almost never replay them. Why is everyone having a meltdown over this shit?

She opens a hotel in hopes that patients will be "checking out" into Heaven. Hazbin Hotel Pilot: https://www.you

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Making use of LLMs
entry.content: I'm developing lore for a fiction thing, doesn't really matter what. It's about the most flexible, error-tolerant endeavor you can imagine. Mistakes into miracles-type nonsense, almost. And yet, I am struggling to apply LLMs to the task in any productive way. For example, I write up some new facts and make it rework documents from the project files, so the old ones can be replaced with updated ones. I go >okay, there's cyclops in the mountains. They got one eye each and are big. the LLM will cra

entry.title: /b/ Random: So I'm pretty sure E is actually Funnyjunk's Admin. Think about it, Admin is abs…
entry.content: So I'm pretty sure E is actually Funnyjunk's Admin. Think about it, Admin is absolutely terrified of turds and barf, including the glorious logs King Andy produces daily, and will viciously persecute any who post them on his site. And on 4chan, where no such persecution exists, E is trying to convince the mods to institute similar authoritarian measures. So they're one and the same. What say you, fellow logposters?

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Why hasn't LaTeX been replaced with something better yet?
entry.content: >install 7 GB of tooling to write a paragraph >compiler error points to line 7, problem is on line 412: missing one brace >undefined control sequence, refuses to elaborate >package conflict discovered only after 20-minute compile >overfull \hbox >spend longer aligning a figure than doing the research >lualatex, xelatex, pdflatex literally wtf https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=KhW wuQ7qf5A
